activities_projects.title: Psychological Character Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the psychological traits and inner conflicts of Lomov and Natalia.
steps: Identify moments of insecurity, pride, and anxiety in both characters
Explain how these traits influence dialogue and conflict
Write a structured analytical essay on character psychology
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand character motivations at a deeper level.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual support, and clarity of reasoning

activities_projects.title: Satire and Humor Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Critically examine how Chekhov creates humor and social satire.
steps: Identify 3–4 instances of humor in the play
Explain the literary techniques used (irony, exaggeration, absurdity)
Write a short essay linking humor to social commentary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop advanced literary analysis skills.

activities_projects.title: Conflict Dynamics Research
activities_projects.objective: Investigate escalation and resolution of conflicts in the play.
steps: Select one major conflict scene
Analyze step-by-step how arguments escalate
Suggest alternative ways the conflict could have been resolved and discuss effects
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand conflict dynamics and alternative problem-solving.
activities_projects.evaluation: Analytical depth, logical reasoning, and clarity

activities_projects.title: Modern Adaptation Critique
activities_projects.objective: Reimagine the play in a contemporary setting and analyze impact.
steps: Adapt the play to modern situations (e.g., workplace or college proposal)
Write dialogues and note changes in character behavior
Explain how modern adaptation affects humor and social commentary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop creativity, critical thinking, and comparative skills.

activities_projects.title: Dramatic Monologue Writing
activities_projects.objective: Explore characters’ inner thoughts and conflicts.
steps: Choose either Lomov or Natalia
Write a monologue revealing their thoughts during a conflict scene
Include fears, pride, and intentions in a dramatic voice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand internal conflict and expressive writing.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of insight, creativity, and alignment with character traits

activities_projects.title: Thematic Research Essay
activities_projects.objective: Analyze major themes such as pride, ego, and social manners in depth.
steps: Identify 2–3 themes present in the play
Support each theme with textual examples
Write a well-structured essay connecting themes to human behavior
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance critical thinking and thematic analysis skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Analytical depth, textual support, and clarity of argument

activities_projects.title: Comparative Humor Study
activities_projects.objective: Compare Chekhov’s humor style with another playwright.
steps: Select 3 humorous situations from the play
Compare with similar examples in another play (e.g., Oscar Wilde or George Bernard Shaw)
Write a comparative analysis highlighting techniques and effects
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop comparative literature and analytical skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightfulness, examples, and clarity of comparison

activities_projects.title: Role Reversal Performance
activities_projects.objective: Explore alternative perspectives by reversing character roles.
steps: Perform a key scene with Lomov and Natalia swapping characteristics
Observe how dialogue and humor change
Reflect on the effect of personality traits on conflict
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand the role of personality in conflict and humor.

activities_projects.title: Character Emotion Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Map emotional fluctuations and triggers in the play.
steps: Select key scenes
Identify emotional highs and lows for each character
Draw a color-coded map showing triggers and responses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze emotion, tension, and dramatic structure.

activities_projects.title: Critical Review Article
activities_projects.objective: Write an in-depth review as a literary critic.
steps: Analyze play’s strengths and weaknesses
Discuss characters, humor, and theme effectiveness
Write a critical review suitable for publication
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop advanced writing and critical evaluation skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth, argument clarity, textual references, and professionalism
